macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw Treasury yields climb further with the 10-year hitting 4.67% and the 30-year above 5.14% as bond markets priced in persistent inflation, per the CNBC bond rout article. The S&P 500 slipped 0.7% to 733.73 while oil held above $103 on Hormuz tensions despite Trump postponing an Iran strike. Tech was under pressure ahead of NVDA's Wednesday earnings, and the SOXX semiconductor index dipped 0.06% to 496.74.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $103.81, down 0.3%, as the Hormuz disruption continues to drain accessible inventories and global shipping reroutes, though Trump's delay of a strike eased immediate supply fears. Gold inched up 0.25% to $4,474.50. The DXY held at 99.39.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain CAUTION under the dual NIM-compression headwind, with US10Y at 4.67% well above the 4.20% threshold and MAS S$NEER unchanged. OCBC has outperformed (R:R 0.8:1 vs. DBS 0.7:1) on better cost control, but the cluster offers no entry until rates or MAS policy shifts.
singapore.key_development: Singapore banks' NIM relief gate — US10Y below 4.20% — remains firmly closed at 4.67%.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is deep in hard-block territory with 13 names extended, including NVDA ahead of its earnings binary. SOXX at 496.74 (-0.06%) reflects a cautious pre-print posture. NVDA's print tomorrow is the cluster-wide catalyst — a guide raise could reset R:R for the group, while a miss would extend the caution.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically frozen by extension and binary risk
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA reports after close Wednesday — a beat-and-raise would upgrade the cluster’s entry prospects; flat guidance would extend the hard-block regime.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is uniformly CAUTION: AMZN and GOOG are hard-blocked above 8% and 15% respectively, while MSFT, though closest to its 50-day at 4.5% above, still has a 0.4:1 R:R. The cluster lacks a near-term entry unless the NVDA print sparks a broad tech pullback that brings these names to their 50-days.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally sound, tactically blocked — capex narratives intact but prices extended
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Gates stake sale and OpenAI circularity concerns add caution despite the Azure revenue run.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment mirrors LITE's — Coherent is the other leg of the AI optics supply chain.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's post-NVDA pullback stabilizes above $830 and Coherent's 60-day average is tested.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture — surfaces as an on-device AI inference play alongside NVDA data-center buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if a market-wide selloff after NVDA earnings pushes QCOM down 8-10% to its 200-day average.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI networking demand from hyperscalers ties directly to NVDA and LITE's optical interconnect ramp; ANET benefits from 800G/1.6T datacenter switching.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA earnings confirm networking demand acceleration and ANET pulls back to its 50-day.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with 10 ships crossing daily vs. 5-7 in prior weeks — oil inventory drain and shipping reroutes continue (per Vanda Insights, May 18).
US sanctions 19 vessels tied to Iranian oil exports via OFAC, escalating the 'Economic Fury' campaign (per Treasury Dept., May 19).
Treasury yields surge: 10-year at 4.67%, 30-year above 5.14% as bond markets price in persistent inflation and a potential Fed rate hike by December (per Forbes, May 20).
US-China tariff deal yields a preliminary agreement to lower some tariffs, with China committing to buy 200 Boeing jets — but chip export controls remain unaddressed (per Politico, May 18).
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil keeps WTI above $100 and Treasury yields high, compressing tech multiples and hard-blocking the semis cluster. The Singapore banks face dual NIM pressure from high US rates and a strong SGD.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump postponed a planned Iran strike (per LA Times, May 18), briefly easing oil but yields continued to climb.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; the dominant posture is waiting for the NVDA earnings binary and a potential pullback in extended semis names — no new entries until one of these resets occurs.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — heavy extension and binary risk
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 13 of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, with NVDA's earnings tomorrow the make-or-break event for the semis and BigTech clusters.

watch_for_entry.note: RSI 33.7, approaching oversold. Wait for reclaim of $6.98 on volume; stop below $6.03. ADR risk demands starter sizing.
